message.content:
<deliberation>
I will evaluate each main entry node (REG-101, REG-013, REG-091) against the provided policy chunk, using any provided referenced nodes. Only REG-013 has provided regulatory text; it references paragraphs 2–5, and only paragraph 2 is supplied as REG-014. Per the Evidence Requirement, I will only assert deviations where I can quote the exact regulatory text that demonstrates a contradiction, narrowing, or other difference; otherwise, I will mark Non-Deviation (including omissions). For REG-101 and REG-091, no regulatory text is provided, so I must classify them as Non-Deviation with no deviations.

For REG-013:
- I will include REG-014 as a considered reference because Art 10(1) explicitly points to paras 2–5.
- Deviation 1 (Severity): The regulation scopes obligations to “High-risk AI systems,” but the policy applies them to “All AI systems.” This is over-compliance (broader scope). I can quote Art 10(1) and the policy line “Applies to: All AI systems.”
- Deviation 2 (Data): Art 10(2)(e), read with the opening sentence of 10(2), requires “an assessment of the availability, quantity and suitability of the data sets that are needed” across training, validation, and testing data sets subject to data governance. The policy confines this assessment to cases where “existing data sets” are used “to train the system,” thereby narrowing both the type of data sets (existing) and the phase (training), whereas the regulation’s requirement applies more broadly. I can quote Art 10(2) including item (e) and the policy sentence beginning “If you plan to use existing data sets to train the system...”.

I will not flag omissions (e.g., missing references to “updating,” “bias mitigation,” “origin of data,” etc.) as deviations per the instruction to treat omissions as Non-Deviation. I will also avoid claiming an “execution style” deviation about documentation location (Impact Assessment), since I cannot quote regulatory text that prescribes or forbids a specific documentation medium; absence of such a prescription is not quotable evidence.

For REG-101 and REG-091, I cannot extract any regulatory text; per rule 4.1, I must classify as Non-Deviation.
</deliberation>
```json
[
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-101",
    "clause": "Unknown (text not provided)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-013",
    "clause": "Art10(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-014"],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "High-risk AI systems which make use of techniques involving the training of AI models with data shall be developed on the basis of training, validation and testing data sets that meet the quality criteria referred to in paragraphs 2 to 5 whenever such data sets are used.",
        "policy_quote": "Applies to: All AI systems.",
        "mismatch": "The regulation limits the requirement to high-risk AI systems, whereas the policy applies it to all AI systems (over-compliance in scope).",
        "analysis": "Article 10(1) scopes obligations to high-risk AI systems; the policy extends these governance requirements to all AI systems, which is stricter than required."
      },
      {
        "deviation_type": "Data deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "Training, validation and testing data sets shall be subject to data governance and management practices appropriate for the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system. Those practices shall concern in particular: (a) the relevant design choices; (b) data collection processes and the origin of data, and in the case of personal data, the original purpose of the data collection; (c) relevant data-preparation processing operations, such as annotation, labelling, cleaning, updating, enrichment and aggregation; (d) the formulation of assumptions, in particular with respect to the information that the data are supposed to measure and represent; (e) an assessment of the availability, quantity and suitability of the data sets that are needed; (f) examination in view of possible biases that are likely to affect the health and safety of persons, have a negative impact on fundamental rights or lead to discrimination prohibited under Union law, especially where data outputs influence inputs for future operations; (g) appropriate measures to detect, prevent and mitigate possible biases identified according to point (f); (h) the identification of relevant data gaps or shortcomings that prevent compliance with this Regulation, and how those gaps and shortcomings can be addressed.",
        "policy_quote": "If you plan to use existing data sets to train the system, assess the quantity and suitability of available data sets that will be needed by the system in relation to the data requirements defined in A4.1. A4.3 Document this assessment in the Impact Assessment.",
        "mismatch": "The regulation requires assessing availability, quantity and suitability of all needed data sets (covering training, validation and testing), but the policy limits this assessment to cases using existing datasets to train the system.",
        "analysis": "Art 10(2) applies governance practices, including the (e) assessment, to training, validation and testing data sets that are needed; the policy narrows this to existing data sets and to the training phase, thereby restricting the data categories and processing stages covered by the assessment."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-091",
    "clause": "Unknown (text not provided)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  }
]
```
